data.title: Kyndryl partners with Nova Intelligence for AI-powered SAP modernization approach. Collaboration announced in NY on Jan 21, 2026. (Character count: 114)
data.description: Kyndryl is working with Nova Intelligence to introduce the Clean Field approach, enhancing customer support during SAP transformations. The collaboration was announced in New York on Jan. 21, 2026. Kyndryl, listed on NYSE as KD, specializes in critical enterprise technology solutions.

data.title: Bitcoin price aims for $95,000 by February, RSI at 44.66. Institutional targets at $165K-$200K year-end, facing $92,680 resistance in consolidation.
data.description: The current Relative Strength Index (RSI) for Bitcoin stands at 44.66, indicating a neutral stance. Institutional analysts have set ambitious price targets for Bitcoin, ranging between $165,000 and $200,000 by the end of the year. However, in the short term, Bitcoin is encountering strong resistance at $92,680 as it goes through a consolidation phase. Investors are keeping a close eye on these key levels as Bitcoin's price dynamics continue to evolve.

data.title: Treasury bond auction for RIKB 28 1115 and RIKS 37 0115 with ISIN numbers on auction date, 10:30-11:00 am. 10% additional purchase applicable.
data.description: During the upcoming auction between 10:30 am and 11:00 am, the Government Debt Management will auction Treasury bonds of the Series, with specific ISIN numbers and Maturity Dates as outlined in the table. Payments for these bonds are required to be received by the Central Bank by 14:00 on the Settlement Date. The Bonds will be delivered electronically on the same day. Eligibility for purchasing an additional 10% is subject to Article 6 of the General Terms of Auction for Treasury bonds.

data.title: Adams & Reese adds 9 former attorneys from McGlinchey Stafford, led by Rudy Aguilar and Jean-Paul Perrault.
data.description: In a recent development on January 21, 2026, Adams & Reese disclosed the recruitment of nine ex-lawyers from McGlinchey Stafford, marking their official incorporation on Friday, January 16, 2026. The team, headed by Rudy Aguilar and Jean-Paul "J-P" Perrault, comprises the majority of the corporate and real estate divisions at the firm.

data.title: Stephen McMaster appointed as Chief Information Security Officer at Western Alliance Bank, bringing 25+ years of experience in financial tech and security. He will be based in Phoenix.
data.description: Western Alliance Bank, a leading financial institution listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol WAL, has named Stephen McMaster as its new Chief Information Security Officer (CISO). With over 25 years of experience in the financial services sector, McMaster has held key roles in cybersecurity, risk management, and technology control at major financial organizations. Situated in Phoenix, his appointment signifies the bank's commitment to enhancing its cybersecurity measures. McMaster's extensive background in establishing and overseeing information security systems will play a pivotal role in fortifying the bank's digital defenses.

data.title: Alexander’s, Inc. to release Q4 earnings on Feb 9, 2026, after the NYSE opens. Annual report for 2025 to be filed with SEC.
data.description: A company based in Paramus, N.J., Alexander’s, Inc. (NYSE: ALX), shared plans to submit its annual report on Form 10-K for the year concluding on December 31, 2025, with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission. The organization is set to unveil its financial results for the fourth quarter and full year on Monday, February 9, 2026, before trading commences on the New York Stock Exchange.

data.title: In 2026, travel costs vary: Prices up for luxury travelers compared to last year, but budget travelers may see savings.
data.description: High-end travelers will experience an increase in expenses compared to the previous year, with costs exceeding those from before. On the other hand, budget-conscious travelers may find some relief as prices are expected to be more affordable.

data.title: Netflix defends against bear case, argues critics overlook key metrics. Concerns around total view hours and acquisition rumors. CEO dismisses engagement worries.
data.description: Netflix Inc (NASDAQ: NFLX) is responding to critics who suggest weakening fundamentals, asserting that the issue lies in the metrics being used to assess the business rather than the business itself. During its fourth quarter earnings call, Netflix executives highlighted that total viewing hours are being overly emphasized, while key indicators for long-term value creation are being overlooked. Concerns have been raised about stagnant or decreasing total viewing hours, with speculation about a potential Warner Bros.–HBO acquisition as a means to boost engagement. Co-CEO Greg Peters refuted this notion during Netflix's earnings call, arguing that it misinterprets the company's strategy and outlook. For more details, visit the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: CACI International Inc likely to report higher Q4 earnings; analysts forecast $6.49/share, revenue at $2.28 billion. Awarded $416M task order for US Navy. Stock closed at $629.14.
data.description: CACI International Inc (NYSE: CACI) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings after the market closes on Jan. 21. Analysts predict that the Reston, Virginia-headquartered company will report earnings of $6.49 per share for the quarter, an increase from $5.95 per share in the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is expected to be around $2.28 billion, up from $2.1 billion a year ago. The company recently secured a $416 million task order to enhance Navy systems. CACI International's stock price dropped 1% to $629.14 at the close of trading on Tuesday. For more details and analyst ratings, visit the Benzinga Pro platform.

data.title: SEALSQ to establish 300M unit per year Post-Quantum Semiconductor Center in India with Govt of Gujarat.
data.description: SEALSQ Corp (NASDAQ: LAES), a subsidiary of WISeKey International Holding AG, WISeKey International Holding Ltd (SIX: WIHN, NASDAQ: WKEY), specializing in Semiconductors, PKI, and Post-Quantum technology products, will partner with the Government of Gujarat and Kaynes SemiCon Private Limited, a subsidiary of Kaynes Technology India Limited. Together, they have signed a Non-binding Memorandum of Understanding to establish India's inaugural Secure Semiconductor Design, Test, and Personalization Center specifically for post-quantum cryptography technologies. This collaboration marks a significant milestone in the cybersecurity sector.

data.title: eHub names Thomas Leishman as Chief AI Officer in its bid to enhance AI-driven logistics.
data.description: In Sandy, Utah, on January 21, 2026, eHub, a prominent figure in Carrier Orchestration and Fulfillment Intelligence, revealed the selection of Thomas Leishman as their new Chief AI Officer (CAIO). Assuming the position, Thomas will be responsible for leading eHub's AI strategy, focusing on fostering innovation throughout the company.

data.title: Aluma Trailers acquired by Novae with Northborne Partners serving as exclusive financial advisor.
data.description: On January 21, 2026, in Minneapolis, Northborne Partners provided financial advisory services to KLM Acquisition Corporation, also known as Aluma Trailers, in its acquisition by Novae, a company owned by Brightstar Capital Partners. The deal involved undisclosed financial figures and marks a significant move in the industry.

data.title: Richmond American Homes introduces Kennington community in Aylett with 5 floor plans in Aylett, VA.
data.description: Kennington, located in Aylett, Virginia, features five meticulously designed floor plans showcasing exquisite designer details. Richmond American Homes of Virginia, Inc., a subsidiary of Sekisui House U.S., Inc., proudly presents this new development. For more information on Kennington, visit RichmondAmerican.com/Kennington.

data.title: U.S. trade proclamation emphasizes importance of domestic rare earths supply chain; issued on Jan 14, 2026. Aimed at strengthening national defense & advanced manufacturing.
data.description: In Denver on January 21, 2026, American Rare Earths acknowledged the importance of a recent U.S. trade proclamation from January 14, 2026. The proclamation aims to address imports of processed critical minerals and derivatives under Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act. It seeks to bolster domestic supply chains, minimize strategic vulnerabilities in national defense, and enhance advanced manufacturing supply chains. American Rare Earths is listed as ASX: ARR, OTCQX: ARRNF, and ADR: AMRRY, and is responding to the proclamation issued by adjusting imports to contribute to these goals.

data.title: Healthcare IT Market to reach $961.26 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 14.9% from 2025's $480.49 billion.
data.description: A report from MarketsandMarkets™ reveals that the Healthcare IT Market is expected to double in value, increasing from approximately USD 480.49 billion in 2025 to USD 961.26 billion by 2030, representing a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 14.9%. The report includes extensive market data, with 637 tables and 64 figures across 633 pages.

data.title: Improved golf tee for cold weather designed by InventHelp inventors to prevent cracking, shattering.
data.description: In Pittsburgh on January 21, 2026, two inventors from Coventry, Rhode Island unveiled THE ALL NEW GOLF TEE, specifically engineered for cold weather play. The innovative tee aims to address common issues faced during chilly rounds of golf by eliminating cracking or shattering. This new design showcases enhancements to durability, promising golfers a reliable option. The creators have high hopes for their creation, confident that it will revolutionize the golfing experience, with potential benefits for players facing challenging weather conditions.
